metadata.preview:
# Create T3 App

This is a [T3 Stack](https://create.t3.gg/) project bootstrapped with `create-t3-app`.

## What's next? How do I make an app with this?

We try to keep this project as simple as possible, so you can start with just the scaffolding we set up for you, and add additional things later when they become necessary.

If you are not familiar with the different technologies used in this project, please refer to the respective docs. If you still are in the wind, please join our [Discord](https://t3.gg/discord) and ask for help.

- [Next.js](https://nextjs.org)
- [NextAuth.js](https://next-auth.js.org)
- [Prisma](https://prisma.io)
- [Drizzle](https://orm.drizzle.team)
- [Tailwind CSS](https://tailwindcss.com)
- [tRPC](https://trpc.io)

## Learn More

To learn more about the [T3 Stack](https://create.t3.gg/), take a look at the following resources:

state.title: Read inngest functions
Read prompts.ts

part.text:
Classification: AGENT
Confidence: high

Agent Signals:
- Tool usage directed by an LLM
- Multi-step execution loop (up to 5 steps)
- Nested reasoning loop within a tool

Evidence Files:
- src/app/api/chat/route.ts
- src/ai-core/tools/search-connections.ts
- src/components/chat/chat.tsx

Frameworks Detected:
- Vercel AI SDK (ai, @ai-sdk/react)

Reasoning:
The repository implements an AI agent using the Vercel AI SDK. The main chat endpoint (`src/app/api/chat/route.ts`) utilizes `streamText` with a multi-step execution loop (`stopWhen: stepCountIs(5)`) and provides a `searchConnections` tool. This allows the LLM to autonomously decide when to call the tool and process its output. Furthermore, the tool itself (`src/ai-core/tools/search-connections.ts`) executes a secondary LLM call to reason about the relevance of the retrieved connections before returning the results to the primary agent loop. This combination of autonomous tool usage, multi-step execution, and reasoning loops clearly classifies the repository as an agent system.
